rulings.Case Title: JAMES RONAN ET AL VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 13, 2024, in Department 301, Line 1. Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Advancing the Current Trial Date with Preference, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed. 1. The July 8, 2024 trial date is advanced to June 3, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206. a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is June 12, 2024. b. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. 2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is May 28, 2024. a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. b. The Court will allow eight summary judgment/adjudication motions to be calendared in Asbestos Law and Motion for this action, unless the Court finds good cause for additional summary judgment/adjudication motions to be filed. c. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. 3. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. b. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. 4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. 5. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 17, 2024. 6. Expert discovery cut-off date is May 31, 2024. 7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 13, 2024. a. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. 8. No later than February 27, 2024: a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. b. Defendants must also provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Defendant's affirmative defenses. c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. (Tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES RONAN ET AL VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR TENTATIVE RULING ENTRY PURPOSES ONLY)
rulings.Rulings: (Part 2 of 2) (Tentative ruling continued from previous entry) 9. A further case management conference is set for April 9, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. a. There is no need for any party to file any case management conference statement. b. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. c. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. 10. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, May 30, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. a. Plaintiffs are to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than April 19, 2024. Responses to the demand are due no later than May 3, 2023. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to the Court's ADR Coordinator via electronic mail at ADRCoordinator@sftc.org by May 24, 2024 at 12:00 p.m. If a hearing is requested, it will be on February 13, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 301, the Hon. Rochelle C. East presiding. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances must be arranged through CourtCall (1-888-88-COURT). No prior notice or permission is required. If a party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter, who must be licensed under Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 8016. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. The prevailing party must prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. If contested, the proposed order must be submitted to the Court after the hearing. If not contested, the proposed order may be submitted before or after the hearing. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PAGANO JR ET AL VS. AKEBONO BRAKE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Kias Written Discovery
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, February 13, 2024, in Department 301, Line 1. Defendant Kia America, Inc.'s Motion to Compel Further Responses to Kia's Written Discovery is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. Defendant failed to comply with Local Rule 20.1(C). If a hearing is requested, it will be on February 13, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 301, the Hon. Rochelle C. East presiding. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances must be arranged through CourtCall (1-888-88-COURT). No prior notice or permission is required. If a party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter, who must be licensed under Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 8016. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. The prevailing party must prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. If contested, the proposed order must be submitted to the Court after the hearing. If not contested, the proposed order may be submitted before or after the hearing. = (301/RCE)
